inputs.name: input_description
inputs.value: Select the meeting to prepare you for

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Extract the meeting ID from the following input: <input>{{selected_meeting}}</input>

For instance, if the input is "Important Meeting [startDate-endDate] (#123456)", return back only 123456, nothing else.

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Extract the title of the event and date for a re: email subject line from the following selected meeting input: <input>{{selected_meeting}}</input>

For instance, if the input is "Important Meeting [startDate-endDate] (#123456)", return back only something along the lines of "Re: Important Meeting (computed date in EST)"

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
EXTRACT from Google Calendar event:
  - Title, Description/notes
  - Start time, end time, duration
  - Attendees list (all)
  - Conference/meeting link, Location
  - Recurrence pattern, Organizer

The current user is {{user_email}} {{_google_email}}.

PROCESS attendees:
  - Split into internal vs external (by email domain)
  - Identify organizer
  - Mark required vs optional
  - Extract company domains from external attendees

<google_calendar_event>
{{enriched_calendar_event}}
</google_calendar_event>

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "event": {"title": "string", "description": "string or null", "start_time": "ISO 8601", "end_time": "ISO 8601", "duration_minutes": "number", "location": {"type": "physical | virtual | hybrid | none", "address": "string or null"}, "recurrence": {"is_recurring": "boolean", "pattern": "string"}, "conference_link": "string or null", "organizer": {"name": "string", "email": "string", "domain": "string"}},
  "attendees": {"internal": [{"name": "string", "email": "string", "attendance_type": "required | optional"}], "external": [{"name": "string", "email": "string", "domain": "string", "company": "string", "attendance_type": "required | optional"}], "summary": {"total_count": "number", "internal_count": "number", "external_count": "number", "external_domains": [], "external_companies": []}}
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Extract the meeting host/organizer (the seller) from the calendar event.

INPUT:
<calendar_event>{{processed_gcal_event}}</calendar_event>

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"seller_name": "string", "seller_email": "string", "seller_company": "string", "is_identified": true/false}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
ANALYZE the meeting to identify signals from title and description.

INPUT: <event>{{processed_gcal_event}}</event>

Keyword patterns: demo/product tour → Demo, intro/discovery → First contact, technical/architecture → Technical, pricing/proposal → Negotiation, renewal/expansion → Renewal, QBR/quarterly → Strategic review, kickoff/onboarding → Onboarding, issue/urgent → Problem solving

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"meeting_signals": {"primary_signal": {"type": "string", "confidence": "number", "matched_keywords": []}, "secondary_signals": []}, "context_summary": {"meeting_stage": "pre_sales | onboarding | active_customer | renewal | support", "urgency_level": "low | medium | high | critical", "key_topics": []}}

inputs.name: instructions
instructions
inputs.value:
IDENTIFY meeting relationships by analyzing current event against calendar history.

INPUT:
<event>{{processed_gcal_event}}</event>
<topic_signals>{{topic_signals}}</topic_signals>
<calendar_history>{{past_calendar_events}}</calendar_history>

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"relationship_status": {"is_first_meeting": "boolean", "total_previous_meetings": "number", "last_interaction_date": "string or null", "relationship_stage": "new | early | developing | established | mature"}, "topic_continuity": {"related_meetings": [], "has_progression": "boolean"}, "insights": {"relationship_summary": "string", "key_patterns": [], "recommendations": []}}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
CLASSIFY the meeting using multi-factor scoring.

INPUT:
<event>{{processed_gcal_event}}</event>
<topic_signals>{{topic_signals}}</topic_signals>
<relationships>{{meeting_relationships}}</relationships>

MEETING TYPES: first_contact, product_demo, technical_deep_dive, negotiation, renewal, qbr_strategic_review, onboarding, problem_solving, check_in

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"classification": {"primary_type": "string", "primary_confidence": "number (0-100)", "alternative_type": "string or null"}, "attributes": {"buyer_stage": "awareness | consideration | decision | retention", "urgency": "high | medium | low", "complexity": "simple | complex", "relationship_maturity": "cold | warm | hot"}}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Synthesize the seller's LinkedIn profile into useful context.

INPUT:
<seller_linkedin>{{seller_linkedin_profile}}</seller_linkedin>
<seller_info>{{seller_info}}</seller_info>

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"seller_profile": {"name": "string", "title": "string", "company": "string", "linkedin_url": "string"}, "what_they_sell": {"product_or_service": "string", "value_proposition": "string", "target_customers": "string", "key_differentiators": []}, "seller_background": {"expertise": [], "experience_summary": "string", "credibility_signals": []}, "meeting_relevance": {"why_theyre_the_right_person": "string", "topics_they_can_speak_to": []}}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Extract the PRIMARY external company from the meeting attendees.

INPUT: <external_attendees>{{classified_attendees}}</external_attendees>

Identify the primary company (most attendees, or first if tied). Clean the domain (remove mail., email. prefixes).

OUTPUT (JSON):
{"company_name": "string", "domain": "string", "is_personal_email": "boolean", "primary_contact_email": "string", "primary_contact_name": "string", "attendee_count": "number", "attendee_names": [], "confidence": "high | medium | low"}

inputs.name: instructions
instructions
inputs.value:
Generate the Header and At-a-Glance Overview section for the meeting prep document.

The user receiving this prep is: {{user.context.first_name}} {{user.context.last_name}}

INPUT:
<meeting>{{processed_gcal_event}}</meeting>
<classification>{{meeting_classification}}</classification>
<primary_contact>{{target_company_identity}}</primary_contact>
<relationships>{{meeting_relationships}}</relationships>

## VOICE GUIDELINES (from Agent.ai Brand Voice)

Write like a friendly expert briefing a colleague. The tone is warm, clear, curious, and human.

DO:
- Use contractions (you'll, don't, here's, it's)
- Write conversationally, one-to-one
- Explain the why, not just the what
- Add small moments of delight
- Be specific with facts and dates
- Stay grounded but optimistic

DON'T:
- Corporate jargon (synergy, leverage, circle back, align, bandwidth)
- Buzzwords (revolutionary, game-changing, cutting-edge, seamless)
- Sarcasm or silliness
- Condescension or talking down
- Filler words (very, really, quite, just, actually)
- Passive voice when active is clearer

VIBE: "Pull up a chair. Let's figure this out together."

## FIXED LABELS (use exactly as written)

The output must use these exact labels:
- "Meeting type + duration"
- "Primary contact"
- "Conversation opener"
- "TL;DR"

## CONTENT GUIDANCE

1. Header info:
   - title: "[Company Name] + [Your Company]" format
   - tags: Array of 3-4 relevant tags (meeting type, duration, purpose keywords)
   - objective: One warm sentence about what you want to accomplish

2. Greeting: Personalized intro like "Hi [First Name], here's a quick, focused prep to help you run a strong, confident sales call with [Company]."

3. Meeting type + duration: e.g., "Product demo and discovery (30 mins)"

4. Primary contact: Format as "Name — Title at Company"

5. Conversation opener: A specific, relevant opener based on recent signals. NO meta-instructions like "(use verbatim)". Just provide a natural opener they can use.

6. TL;DR: One sentence summarizing what you want to learn and accomplish, written warmly. Example: "Validate the challenges they're running into as they expand into new regions, show how our automation supports territory routing, and identify one or two quick wins we could deliver during a trial."

OUTPUT (JSON):
{
  "header": {
    "title": "string ([Company] + [Your Company] format)",
    "tags": ["string", "string", "string"],
    "objective": "string (warm, human objective)"
  },
  "greeting": "string (personalized intro)",
  "brief": {
    "meeting_type_duration": "string",
    "primary_contact": "string (Name — Title at Company)",
    "conversation_opener": "string (specific and relevant, no meta-instructions)",
    "tldr": "string (warm, human summary)"
  }
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Generate the Who You're Meeting section for attendees.

INPUT:
<external_profiles>{{external_person_profiles}}</external_profiles>
<internal_summary>{{internal_team_summary}}</internal_summary>
<meeting_classification>{{meeting_classification}}</meeting_classification>

## VOICE GUIDELINES (from Agent.ai Brand Voice)

Write like a friendly expert briefing a colleague. The tone is warm, clear, curious, and human.

DO:
- Use contractions (you'll, don't, here's, it's)
- Write conversationally, one-to-one
- Explain the why, not just the what
- Add small moments of delight
- Be specific with facts and dates
- Stay grounded but optimistic

DON'T:
- Corporate jargon (synergy, leverage, circle back, align, bandwidth)
- Buzzwords (revolutionary, game-changing, cutting-edge, seamless)
- Sarcasm or silliness
- Condescension or talking down
- Filler words (very, really, quite, just, actually)
- Passive voice when active is clearer

VIBE: "Pull up a chair. Let's figure this out together."

## FIXED LABELS (use exactly as written)

For each attendee profile, use these exact field labels:
- **Background:**
- **What matters:**
- **How they tend to show up:**
- **Role in the decision:**
- **Potential connection points:**
- **Likely questions or pushback:**
- **Conversation opener:**

For other attendees section:
- **Other attendees to keep in mind:**
- **Quick note for the opening:**

## CONTENT GUIDANCE

For the PRIMARY CONTACT (most senior/important attendee):
1. Name and title prominently displayed
2. LinkedIn link if available
3. Background: 2-3 sentences about professional history, keep it conversational
4. What matters: Their priorities and what they care about
5. How they tend to show up: Communication/decision style (analytical, skeptical, direct, etc.)
6. Role in the decision: Their authority and influence (budget owner, final call, influencer)
7. Potential connection points: Recent LinkedIn activity with specific dates if available
8. Likely questions or pushback: Objections to anticipate, concerns they might raise
9. Conversation opener: A specific opener tailored to this person

For OTHER ATTENDEES:
- Brief bullet list with name, title, and one-line note about their perspective
- Example: "Jennifer Jones (Sales Manager, West Region) brings the end-user perspective."

For QUICK NOTE FOR THE OPENING:
- Practical tip about confirming roles at the start of the call

OUTPUT (JSON):
{
  "primary_contact": {
    "name": "string",
    "title": "string",
    "company": "string",
    "linkedin_url": "string or null",
    "background": "string (2-3 sentences, warm tone)",
    "what_matters": "string",
    "how_they_show_up": "string",
    "role_in_decision": "string",
    "connection_points": "string (with dates if available)",
    "likely_pushback": "string",
    "conversation_opener": "string"
  },
  "other_attendees": [
    {"name": "string", "role_note": "string (title + brief perspective note)"}
  ],
  "opening_tip": "string (quick note for confirming roles)"
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Generate the About the Company section (this appears inside Who You're Meeting).

INPUT:
<company_intelligence>{{company_intelligence}}</company_intelligence>
<target_company>{{target_company_identity}}</target_company>
<meeting_classification>{{meeting_classification}}</meeting_classification>

## VOICE GUIDELINES (from Agent.ai Brand Voice)

Write like a friendly expert briefing a colleague. The tone is warm, clear, curious, and human.

DO:
- Use contractions (you'll, don't, here's, it's)
- Write conversationally, one-to-one
- Explain the why, not just the what
- Add small moments of delight
- Be specific with facts and dates
- Stay grounded but optimistic

DON'T:
- Corporate jargon (synergy, leverage, circle back, align, bandwidth)
- Buzzwords (revolutionary, game-changing, cutting-edge, seamless)
- Sarcasm or silliness
- Condescension or talking down
- Filler words (very, really, quite, just, actually)
- Passive voice when active is clearer

VIBE: "Pull up a chair. Let's figure this out together."

## FIXED LABELS (use exactly as written)

- **Company snapshot**
- **Recent signals**
- **What this signals**
- **Where things are straining**
- **How to frame the conversation**

## CONTENT GUIDANCE

1. Company snapshot: 2-3 sentences covering size, industry, what they do, key stats. Keep it conversational.
   Example: "Mid-market SaaS company doing roughly $45M in ARR, selling project management software to construction firms. Sales team of 45 across three regions."

2. Recent signals: Bullet list of 2-4 recent events with source and date
   Example: "Announced a $30M Series B (TechCrunch, Jan 3)"

3. What this signals: Brief interpretation of what the signals mean
   Example: "Rapid expansion, fresh capital, and aggressive hiring goals."

4. Where things are straining: Pain points or challenges they're likely facing
   Example: "Territory routing is breaking down as the team grows. Lead assignment is still manual."

5. How to frame the conversation: Positioning advice for anchoring the discussion
   Example: "They're in hypergrowth, but their systems haven't caught up. Anchor around what works in the next 30 days."

OUTPUT (JSON):
{
  "company_name": "string",
  "snapshot": "string (2-3 sentences, conversational)",
  "recent_signals": [
    {"signal": "string", "source": "string", "date": "string"}
  ],
  "what_this_signals": "string (interpretation)",
  "where_things_strain": "string (pain points)",
  "how_to_frame": "string (positioning advice)"
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Generate the What to Know Going In and Questions & Tips sections.

INPUT:
<meeting_classification>{{meeting_classification}}</meeting_classification>
<topic_signals>{{topic_signals}}</topic_signals>
<relationships>{{meeting_relationships}}</relationships>
<company>{{section_company}}</company>
<attendees>{{section_attendees}}</attendees>

## VOICE GUIDELINES (from Agent.ai Brand Voice)

Write like a friendly expert briefing a colleague. The tone is warm, clear, curious, and human.

DO:
- Use contractions (you'll, don't, here's, it's)
- Write conversationally, one-to-one
- Explain the why, not just the what
- Add small moments of delight
- Be specific with facts and dates
- Stay grounded but optimistic

DON'T:
- Corporate jargon (synergy, leverage, circle back, align, bandwidth)
- Buzzwords (revolutionary, game-changing, cutting-edge, seamless)
- Sarcasm or silliness
- Condescension or talking down
- Filler words (very, really, quite, just, actually)
- Passive voice when active is clearer

VIBE: "Pull up a chair. Let's figure this out together."

## FIXED LABELS (use exactly as written)

What to Know Going In section:
- **Why they're here**
- **Key insights**
- **Where the opportunity is**
- **Things to watch out for**

Questions & Tips section:
- #### **Discovery questions to guide the conversation**
- #### **Questions they're likely to ask**
- #### **Conversation starters to help you steer**

## CONTENT GUIDANCE

1. Why they're here: Full explanation of what triggered this meeting, not just a label
   Example: "Inbound demo request after Marcus downloaded our Territory Management white paper."

2. Key insights: 3-4 bullet points of the most important things to know
   Be specific and actionable, not generic

3. Where the opportunity is: One paragraph on the best angle to pursue
   This will be displayed as a SUCCESS (green) highlight box in HTML

4. Things to watch out for: One paragraph on risks and pitfalls to avoid
   This will be displayed as a WARNING (orange) highlight box in HTML

5. Discovery questions: 5-6 specific questions written as complete sentences
   Frame as actual questions they could ask verbatim

6. Questions they're likely to ask: 5-6 questions the prospect might ask
   Include product, pricing, timeline, and reference questions

7. Conversation starters: 2 starters to help redirect or steer conversation
   Natural segues or recovery phrases

OUTPUT (JSON):
{
  "what_to_know": {
    "why_here": "string (FULL explanation, not just a label)",
    "key_insights": ["string", "string", "string"],
    "opportunity": "string (for green highlight box)",
    "watch_out": "string (for orange highlight box)"
  },
  "questions": {
    "discovery": ["string", "string", "string", "string", "string", "string"],
    "likely_questions": ["string", "string", "string", "string", "string"],
    "conversation_starters": ["string", "string"]
  }
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Generate the What Success Looks Like and Final Check sections.

INPUT:
<meeting_classification>{{meeting_classification}}</meeting_classification>
<strategy>{{section_strategy}}</strategy>
<meeting>{{processed_gcal_event}}</meeting>

## VOICE GUIDELINES (from Agent.ai Brand Voice)

Write like a friendly expert briefing a colleague. The tone is warm, clear, curious, and human.

DO:
- Use contractions (you'll, don't, here's, it's)
- Write conversationally, one-to-one
- Explain the why, not just the what
- Add small moments of delight
- Be specific with facts and dates
- Stay grounded but optimistic

DON'T:
- Corporate jargon (synergy, leverage, circle back, align, bandwidth)
- Buzzwords (revolutionary, game-changing, cutting-edge, seamless)
- Sarcasm or silliness
- Condescension or talking down
- Filler words (very, really, quite, just, actually)
- Passive voice when active is clearer

VIBE: "Pull up a chair. Let's figure this out together."

## FIXED LABELS (use exactly as written)

What Success Looks Like section:
- **Primary goal**
- **Backup path**
- **Quick wins you can offer**

Final Check section:
- **What we still need to learn**
- **Best path forward**
- **Things to be mindful of**
- **Logistics**
- ### **Big picture takeaway**
- ### **Best opportunity**
- ### **Biggest risk**

## SUMMARY BOX TYPES (for HTML)

These summary boxes have specific semantic colors:
- Big picture takeaway: Muted gray (#F8FAFC background, #0064C7 label)
- Best opportunity: Success green (#ECFDF5 background, #00C2A8 label)
- Biggest risk: Warning peach (#FEF3EC background, #EF7B44 label)

## CONTENT GUIDANCE

1. Primary goal: Specific, actionable goal in warm, human language
   GOOD: "Align on a 30-day pilot with the West region team (15 users), with a clear success metric."
   BAD: "Secure agreement on a low-friction, timeboxed next step with clear owners."

2. Backup path: What's plan B if primary doesn't work

3. Quick wins: 3 specific offers or value-adds you can provide

4. What we still need to learn: 3 info gaps to fill during the meeting

5. Best path forward: One sentence recommendation

6. Things to be mindful of: Risks or considerations

7. Logistics: Always include these three:
   - Confirm all attendees are on the call
   - Have screen share ready
   - Save five minutes at the end to align on next steps

8. Big picture takeaway: One sentence summarizing the situation

9. Best opportunity: One sentence on biggest upside

10. Biggest risk: One sentence on main risk

11. Closing: Warm, encouraging sign-off
    Example: "You've got this. Listen for what's breaking right now, focus the demo on what actually helps, and aim to lock in the pilot before you wrap."

OUTPUT (JSON):
{
  "success": {
    "primary_goal": "string (warm, specific language)",
    "backup_path": "string",
    "quick_wins": ["string", "string", "string"]
  },
  "final_check": {
    "what_to_learn": ["string", "string", "string"],
    "best_path": "string",
    "things_mindful": "string",
    "logistics": ["string", "string", "string"]
  },
  "summary": {
    "big_picture": "string",
    "best_opportunity": "string",
    "biggest_risk": "string"
  },
  "closing": "string (warm, encouraging)"
}

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Assemble all sections into a structured JSON object for HTML conversion.

Combine all section outputs into a single JSON object. Preserve all content from the input sections exactly as provided. The JSON structure must match the template variables used in the HTML conversion step.

## REQUIRED STRUCTURE

The output must follow this exact structure with all fields populated from the input sections:

    "conversation_opener": "string",

  },

    "primary": {
      "name": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "company": "string",
      "linkedin_url": "string or null",
      "background": "string",
      "what_matters": "string",
      "how_they_show_up": "string",
      "role_in_decision": "string",
      "connection_points": "string",
      "likely_pushback": "string",
      "conversation_opener": "string"
    },
    "others": [{"name": "string", "role_note": "string"}],
    "opening_tip": "string"
  },
  "company": {
    "name": "string",
    "snapshot": "string",
    "recent_signals": [{"signal": "string", "source": "string", "date": "string"}],
    "what_this_signals": "string",
    "where_things_strain": "string",
    "how_to_frame": "string"
  },
  "what_to_know": {
    "why_here": "string",
    "key_insights": ["string"],
    "opportunity": "string",
    "watch_out": "string"
  },
  "questions": {
    "discovery": ["string"],
    "likely_questions": ["string"],
    "conversation_starters": ["string"]
  },
  "success": {
    "primary_goal": "string",
    "backup_path": "string",
    "quick_wins": ["string"]
  },
  "final_check": {
    "what_to_learn": ["string"],
    "best_path": "string",
    "things_mindful": "string",
    "logistics": ["string"]
  },
  "summary": {
    "big_picture": "string",
    "best_opportunity": "string",
    "biggest_risk": "string"
  },
  "closing": "string"
}

NOTES:
- Output ONLY valid JSON, no markdown or explanation
- Preserve all content exactly as provided by the section generation steps
- Map fields from input sections to the output structure as indicated

inputs.name: instructions
inputs.value:
Convert the assembled meeting prep JSON to HTML email format using the EXACT designer template.

INPUT:
<data>{{assembled_document}}</data>

## DESIGN SPECIFICATIONS

### Colors (use these exact values)
- Primary (links, section labels): #0064C7
- Success/Opportunity label: #00C2A8, background: #ECFDF5
- Warning/Risk label: #EF7B44, background: #FEF3EC
- Muted box: background #F8FAFC, label #0064C7
- Page background: #F1F5F9
- Card background: #FFFFFF
- Header bar: #E8F1FB
- Card border: #C1D6EB (1px solid, 14px radius)
- Text primary: #0F172A
- Text secondary: #475569
- Divider: #E2E8F0
- Avatar placeholder: #CBD5E1

### Typography
- Font: 'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif
- Section label: 12px, 700 weight, #0064C7
- Meeting title: 28px, 700 weight, #0F172A
- Field label: 14px, 600 weight, #0F172A
- Field value: 14px, 400 weight, #475569
- Profile name: 18px, 600 weight
- Section intro: 13px, 400 weight, italic, #475569
- Box label: 12px, 700 weight, uppercase, 0.5px letter-spacing
- Box text: 15px, 400 weight, #0F172A

### Card Structure
Every card follows this structure:
1. Section header: background #E8F1FB, padding 14px 22px
2. Section label: 12px, 700 weight, #0064C7
3. Card body: background #FFFFFF, padding 24px 22px

### Spacing
- Container max-width: 640px
- Page padding: 24px 16px
- Card margin-bottom: 16px
- Brief item margin-bottom: 18px
- Profile field margin-bottom: 10px

## FIXED SECTION LABELS (in blue header bars)

Card 1: "Meeting Prep"
Card 2: "At-a-Glance Overview"
Card 3: "Who You're Meeting"
Card 4: "About the Company — [Company Name]"
Card 5: "What to Know Going In"
Card 6: "Questions & Tips"
Card 7: "What Success Looks Like"
Card 8: "Final Check Before the Call"

## HIGHLIGHT BOX STYLES

Opportunity (mint green):
background: #ECFDF5; padding: 16px 20px; border-radius: 8px;
label: font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #00C2A8; text-transform: uppercase;

Risk/Warning (peach):
background: #FEF3EC; padding: 16px 20px; border-radius: 8px;
label: font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #EF7B44; text-transform: uppercase;

Muted (gray):
background: #F8FAFC; padding: 16px 20px; border-radius: 8px;
label: font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #0064C7; text-transform: uppercase;

## 8-CARD STRUCTURE

Generate HTML with exactly 8 cards:

1. Meeting Prep Header - title, tags, objective, greeting
2. At-a-Glance Overview - meeting type, primary contact, opener, TL;DR
3. Who You're Meeting - primary attendee profile with all fields, other attendees box
4. About the Company - snapshot, signals, interpretation, pain points, framing
5. What to Know Going In - why here, key insights, opportunity box (green), risk box (orange)
6. Questions & Tips - discovery questions, likely questions, conversation starters
7. What Success Looks Like - primary goal, backup path, quick wins
8. Final Check - what to learn, best path, logistics, summary boxes (big picture/gray, opportunity/green, risk/orange), closing

## FIELD LABEL MAPPING (use these exact labels when rendering)

Attendee Profile fields:
- background -> "Background:"
- what_matters -> "What matters:"
- how_they_show_up -> "How they tend to show up:"
- role_in_decision -> "Role in the decision:"
- connection_points -> "Potential connection points:"
- likely_pushback -> "Likely questions or pushback:"
- conversation_opener -> "Conversation opener:"

Company fields:
- snapshot -> "Company snapshot"

## OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS

1. Return ONLY valid HTML, no markdown or explanation
2. All styles must be inline (email compatibility)
